b2plus-quotative.title: Reporting What Others Say (дейді, депті)
b2plus-quotative.explanation: To report speech, rumor, or hearsay, Kazakh uses the converb деп ("saying that") after the quoted content, followed by a verb of saying. Дейді reports something as commonly said or believed: Ол ауру дейді (They say he's sick). Депті — built on the reportive past — specifically marks the information as secondhand: Ертеде бір хан болыпты депті (Once there was a khan, so the story goes), common in folk tales.
b2plus-quotative.fact: Депті layers two evidential markers at once — the -ды/-ті reportive past embedded inside деп + депті — which is why it's the signature ending of Kazakh oral storytelling: opening a tale with "...болыпты" instantly signals "this is a story I'm retelling, not something I witnessed myself."

b2plus-comparative-clauses.title: "The More... The More..." Clauses (қаншалықты...соғұрлым)
b2plus-comparative-clauses.explanation: To build "the more X, the more Y" constructions, Kazakh pairs a question-word-based first clause (қаншалықты, "however much") with a correlative second clause introduced by соғұрлым or соншалықты ("to that same extent"): Қаншалықты көп жаттықсаң, соғұрлым жақсы боласың (The more you practise, the better you get).
b2plus-comparative-clauses.fact: This construction already appeared informally at A2 level with соншалықты; at this level the focus is on the fuller, more formal соғұрлым variant, which appears more often in written or rhetorical Kazakh than its everyday counterpart.

dialogue.en: How can one learn a language well?

dialogue.native: Қаншалықты көп сөйлессең, соғұрлым жылдам үйренесің.
dialogue.en: The more you speak it, the faster you learn.

b2plus-impersonal-passive.title: Impersonal and Generalized Statements
b2plus-impersonal-passive.pattern: [verb stem] + passive suffix (-ыл/-іл, -л, -н) without a stated agent
b2plus-impersonal-passive.explanation: Kazakh frequently uses the passive voice to make a general, impersonal statement about how things are done, with no agent implied at all — much like English "it is said that..." or "one doesn't...": Бұл жерде темекі шегілмейді (Smoking is not done here). The construction reads like a rule or social norm rather than a description of a specific event.
b2plus-impersonal-passive.fact: Because Kazakh passive morphology doesn't require naming who performs the action, it's the natural home for signage and regulations — "кіруге болмайды" (entry is not permitted) sounds authoritative and impersonal in exactly the way English needs a separate "one"-construction to achieve.

dialogue.en: No, smoking is not done here.

items.en: They say he's sick.
They say there will be a meeting tomorrow.
Once upon a time there was a rich man, so the story goes.
They say he doesn't know when he'll arrive.
They say the price is expensive.
The khan had three daughters, the tale says.
They say he found a new job.
They say the weather will worsen.
They say he doesn't know this.
There once was a hero, the legend goes.

items.kk: Қаншалықты көп оқысаң, соғұрлым көп білесің.
items.en: The more you read, the more you know.

items.kk: Қаншалықты ерте тұрсаң, соғұрлым көп уақытың болады.
items.en: The earlier you get up, the more time you have.

items.kk: Қаншалықты көп жұмыс істесең, соғұрлым көп табасың.
items.en: The more you work, the more you earn.

items.kk: Қаншалықты жақсы дайындалсаң, соғұрлым сенімді боласың.
items.en: The better you prepare, the more confident you become.

items.kk: Қаншалықты көп сұрақ қойсаң, соғұрлым көп түсінесің.
items.en: The more questions you ask, the more you understand.

items.kk: Қаншалықты жылдам жүрсең, соғұрлым тез жетесің.
items.en: The faster you walk, the sooner you arrive.

items.kk: Қаншалықты көп тәжірибе жинасаң, соғұрлым шебер боласың.
items.en: The more experience you gather, the more skilled you become.

items.kk: Қаншалықты аз ұйықтасаң, соғұрлым нашар сезінесің.
items.en: The less you sleep, the worse you feel.

items.kk: Қаншалықты көп саяхаттасаң, соғұрлым көп үйренесің.
items.en: The more you travel, the more you learn.

items.kk: Қаншалықты қатты тырыссаң, соғұрлым жақсы нәтижеге жетесің.
items.en: The harder you try, the better result you achieve.
Smoking is not done here.
Speaking loudly is not permitted in the office.
This issue is often discussed.
English is not spoken in the shop.
This road is being built.
Photography is not allowed here.
This rule is applied everywhere.
Speaking loudly is not allowed in the library.
This text is translated into many languages.
One must walk carefully here.
